summary: Hard-paywall remodel backend testing complete. 28/28 pytest tests pass (100%) against the public preview URL. Verified: guest+register both return lifetime_ai_calls_remaining=1 / is_premium=false / onboarding_complete=false / quiz_answers=null; no more 7-day auto-trial on register; new POST /api/auth/quiz endpoint stores answers and flips onboarding_complete=true (both full and partial payloads); 1st AI generate succeeds and returns 5 icebreakers + tip with calls_remaining=0; 2nd generate returns 402 with 'Free preview used' / 'subscribe' messaging; 'monthly' plan returns 400 'Invalid plan' (correctly removed); weekly/yearly/lifetime checkout calls return 502 as expected with placeholder STRIPE_API_KEY=sk_test_emergent (Stripe rejects key — code-level config is correct: PRICES dict contains weekly=$6.99 with trial_period_days=3, yearly=$39.99, lifetime=$59.99 mode=payment); guest checkout blocked with 400 'create an account'; library/categories/daily/favorites/history all OK; no _id leaks; quiz does not consume the free AI call.

minor.issue: Returns 502 'Invalid API Key provided: sk_test_****gent' — this is expected per the review_request (placeholder key). Real Stripe key required to fully validate that line_item.recurring.interval and subscription_data.trial_period_days=3 reach Stripe correctly. Code inspection of /app/backend/server.py PRICES + checkout_session() confirms the payload is built correctly.

Doc still mentions '3 AI calls/day' and '7-day free trial on registration' — both are obsolete after this remodel. Main agent should update it to: '1 lifetime AI call for free users; trial is now 3-day, bundled in Stripe weekly subscription.'
QuizIn validates nothing beyond Optional[str]. Out-of-spec values (e.g. age_range='999') are silently stored. Consider Literal['18-24','25-34','35-44','45+'] etc. Not blocking.
Endpoint always sets onboarding_complete=true even when the request body is fully empty ({}). May or may not be desired — flag for product review.

action_items: Replace STRIPE_API_KEY placeholder 'sk_test_emergent' in /app/backend/.env with a real Stripe test secret key so weekly/yearly/lifetime checkout sessions can be E2E-verified (recurring.interval + trial_period_days=3 on weekly).
Update /app/memory/test_credentials.md — remove '3 AI/day' and '7-day trial' lines; replace with the new 1-lifetime-call / 3-day-trial-via-Stripe-weekly model.
Optional: tighten QuizIn with Literal types for age_range/dating_goal/style/meet_location to prevent garbage data.
critical_code_review_comments: PRICES dict cleanly separates subscription vs payment mode and conditionally attaches subscription_data only for subscription plans — good.

is_premium() still references trial_ends_at for backward compat with users created pre-remodel (trial_ends_at can be set by the OLD register flow). Acceptable; trial_ends_at is now always None on new registers.
serialize_user always returns trial_ends_at: None — clients should rely on is_premium + lifetime_ai_calls_remaining instead.
/api/checkout/session swallows stripe.error.AuthenticationError into a generic 502; consider 500 for config errors so monitoring distinguishes ours-vs-Stripe issues.
server.py is ~710 lines now (was 538). Consider splitting into routers/{auth,icebreakers,checkout}.py — exceeds the 700-line guideline.
Guest user document has no email field — serialize_user returns email=None which is fine, but checkout_session passes customer_email=None to Stripe (guests are already blocked, so not reachable).
seed_data_creation: Tests create ephemeral users with email pattern test_<uuid>@example.com and guest sessions. No persistent seed data created. Library seeds (~540 docs) auto-loaded on backend startup.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run: cd /app && pytest backend/tests/test_icebreaker_api.py -v --tb=short. Total ~35s (1 AI Claude call ~10s). Stripe checkout tests accept 200 OR 502 because STRIPE_API_KEY in /app/backend/.env is placeholder 'sk_test_emergent' — once a real test key is in place those tests will validate the live Stripe session URL too. Note: the project previously had 'monthly' plan; it has been deliberately removed and a regression test verifies POST {plan:'monthly'} returns 400.
rca_of_issue: No regressions to RCA — all functional requirements of the hard-paywall remodel verified. The only non-200 endpoints (Stripe checkout 502) are expected per the review request and are gated by environment config, not application code.
